I have been on Mounjaro for about 8 months and a lot of what you said resonates with me. However I haven't worried about who knows I'm on it though I needed something else for my type 2 diabetes as my metformin wasn't enough - but I don't qualify for Mounjaro to be prescribed by my GP. He'd have prescribed a weight neutral tablet but was happy for me to buy it privately. I'm 58 and weighed just under 13 stone and have lost a stone and a half - it's slow but I feel better than I have for ages and clothes shopping is a joy again!

The morning after I injected Mounjaro for the first time, I started to panic: what was I supposed to be doing now?

Am I reading it right that It was cut down in 2024? Why on earth is this being written about now?

Also known as the Burngarth Cockerel after the 18th century Grade II-listed cottage to which it belonged, the sculpted yew - which once reached 30ft tall - was so renowned that it was visited by tourists and schoolchildren and featured on postcards promoting the area. Photographs taken over the decades - with the first recorded image from 1910 - show the cockerel in varying states of leafy plumage but always present in the garden of Bishop Monkton's most iconic property, which is thought to have been built in around 1720. Now, however, in a move which has divided this historic village -mentioned in the Domesday book - Cockadoodle is no more.

  • $20 . A man stopped me at a garage a few years ago. His car had run out of petrol and he’d walked to the garage to buy a can and get some petrol. He had left his wallet in his jacket in his car with his bank cards in. I payed for the petrol AND the can. Then gave him a lift back to his car. He insisted on driving to a cash till to pay me back but by now I was in a hurry so I said just pass on the generosity. It’s your turn and left. This is the first time I’ve ever told anyone. My girlfriend would’ve been mad at me and said I was conned but who cares. It was only £20. I was on my way to a hotel to play piano and I was earning £150 so what’s £20. Made me feel good anyway, and he was a decent sort of bloke, plus he’d walked about a mile in the heat and looked like he was going to collapse, lol.

So???? Are we actually going to let robots compete? No, so what is the hoopla?? Of course a robot will be faster, it doesn't get tired. Once you've solved navigation and handling obstacles, the rest is easy.

During last year's shambolic Beijing robot half marathon, humanoid machines tripped, shuffled, and occasionally shattered into pieces as they collapsed under the strain. But 12 months later, supporters alike looked on in awe as a new generation of speedy robotic racers left the human athletes in the dust. More than 100 robots competed alongside their 12,000 human counterparts, separated by a barrier to prevent collisions. Lightning, the winning bot developed by Chinese smartphone maker Honor, not only beat the best human that day but smashed the world record set only last month.

EXCLUSIVEThe King's ex-drug addict godson - who was disinherited by his furious father Earl Mountbatten - is welcomed back to the family's estate and reinstated as heir to their £100million fortune

So close was Nicholas Knatchbull, the eldest son of Lord and Lady Romsey, to the Royal Family growing up that he holidayed with Princess Diana and young princes William and Harry. Indeed, when the Prince of Wales began at Eton, as King Charles's godson and a few years older than William, Nicholas was entrusted as his mentor to show him school life. But Nicholas's life went spectacularly off the rails with drugs and he was disinherited by his father. Now, in a story of redemption, he has settled down, married and has two children. He has been welcomed back into the family fold and is living on the Broadlands estate, their ancestral home in Romsey, Hampshire.

Mild? It’s bloody freezing

Ample rainfall, combined with a warm start to the season, has created ideal growing conditions for the wildflowers. Temperature records have been broken, with the hottest day since 1946 recorded in the first half of April, reaching 26.6C on April 8 at Kew Gardens, London .

Hugh Jackman and Sutton Foster have the look of love as they cozy up at Sheep Detectives premiere in New York

Hugh Jackman and Sutton Foster made a date out of the Sheep Detectives premiere in New York City on Sunday. The 57-year-old actor and 51-year-old actress - whose reported wedding plans have hit a snag - put on a cozy display while posing for photos together. Jackman looked stylish in a monochrome blue suit and button-up shirt, adding black dress shoes.
  • 233 comments
